backlog.title: Spec-Sync Delta: idle auto-stop / notify_on_final / partial-sync verification + stale comment fix
backlog.description: The PRD was updated to document three already-shipped features (D1: idle auto-stop via asr.auto_stop_idle_seconds, D2: feedback.notify_on_final config gate, D3: record_final writes finalized text into partial field). All three are implemented, committed (git HEAD 367b774), and tested (197 fast tests green). This delta plan is primarily a SPEC-SYNC VERIFICATION: confirm the shipped code matches the updated PRD, fix ONE residual stale comment in config.toml, verify the T4 idle test interaction with the new auto-stop default, and run full regression. No functional code changes are expected or permitted (delta PRD §3.2, §6.3).

milestones.title: Stale comment fix, T4 interaction verification, and full regression
milestones.description: Fix the single stale comment in config.toml:49 (hypr_notify mentions 'partial' which contradicts the anti-spam discipline), verify the T4 idle test (test_idle_and_gpu.sh) still passes with the auto_stop_idle_seconds=30 default, run the complete pytest fast suite as regression, and sweep README.md for any changeset-level doc gaps. The delta PRD explicitly states Mode B docs do not apply (README is already correct), but per SOW §5 a final verification task is included so the implementing agent can confirm.

tasks.title: Fix stale hypr_notify comment in config.toml (remove 'partial')
tasks.description: The comment on config.toml line 49 for the hypr_notify key says 'show a hyprctl notify one-liner for start/partial/final/stop' — the word 'partial' is WRONG. Per PRD §4.5 and §4.6, hyprctl popups fire EXCLUSIVELY on start/final/stop events; partials go to the state file ONLY (the core anti-spam discipline). This is a one-word deletion in a comment — no runtime behavior change. This is Mode A doc-with-work (the comment rides with the config file it documents).

subtasks.title: Remove 'partial/' from the hypr_notify comment in config.toml line 49
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified during research phase — config.toml:49 currently reads: `hypr_notify = true      # show a hyprctl notify one-liner for start/partial/final/stop. Requires Hyprland; harmless elsewhere (the notify call is skipped when false).` The delta PRD §2 identifies this as the ONLY actionable gap: the word 'partial' must be removed because partials NEVER fire hyprctl (PRD §4.6: 'partials go to the state file only'). README.md already states this correctly ('hypr_notify is the master on/off switch'); config.py docstrings are also correct. ONLY config.toml lags.
2. INPUT: The existing config.toml file at /home/dustin/projects/voice-typing/config.toml. The exact line to edit is line 49, the `hypr_notify = true` line with its inline comment.
3. LOGIC: Edit config.toml line 49 ONLY. Change the comment from 'start/partial/final/stop' to 'start/final/stop'. Do NOT change the value (true), do NOT change any other line, do NOT touch config.py or README.md (both already correct per delta PRD §2 verification). The edit is: `start/partial/final/stop` → `start/final/stop`. After the edit, verify with `grep -n 'partial' config.toml` that line 49 no longer contains 'partial' in the hypr_notify comment (the other 'partial' hits on lines 31 and 35 are for realtime_model and realtime_processing_pause — those are CORRECT and must NOT be touched).
4. OUTPUT: config.toml with the corrected comment. No code, no test, no behavior change — comment-only.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] This IS the doc fix — config.toml is the doc being updated. The comment change rides with this subtask. No separate docs subtask needed for this edit.

tasks.title: Verify T4 idle-test interaction with auto-stop and run full regression suite
tasks.description: Two verification objectives: (1) Confirm that test_idle_and_gpu.sh (T4) does not assert listening==true at the end of the 120s idle window — if it did, the auto_stop_idle_seconds=30 default would cause a false failure since the daemon auto-disarms ~30s into the window. (2) Run the complete pytest fast suite to confirm all 197 tests remain green after the comment fix (regression check that no accidental edit broke anything).

subtasks.title: Verify test_idle_and_gpu.sh (T4) does not break with auto_stop_idle_seconds=30 default
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified during research phase — test_idle_and_gpu.sh arms the daemon with `voicectl start`, holds 120s of silence, then checks: (a) capture-pane unchanged (no finals typed), (b) state.json last_final unchanged (no hallucination), (c) `kill -0 $DAEMON_PID` (daemon alive), (d) CPU < 25% of one core. The test does NOT assert `listening==true` at the end of the idle window. With auto_stop_idle_seconds=30.0 (inherited default — the test's temp config only overrides [output] and [feedback]), the daemon auto-disarms ~30s in. Auto-disarm types nothing, doesn't set last_final, doesn't crash, uses negligible CPU → all four T4 assertions still hold. The `voicectl stop` call after the window is idempotent (returns ok:true even if already stopped). VERDICT: T4 passes without modification.
2. INPUT: The file tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh at /home/dustin/projects/voice-typing/tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh.
3. LOGIC: Re-read test_idle_and_gpu.sh and confirm the findings above: (a) the test's temp config override does NOT set auto_stop_idle_seconds (so it inherits 30.0); (b) the test does NOT assert listening==true at the end of the 120s window; (c) the voicectl stop after the window is not expected to fail if already stopped. If ALL THREE confirm, document the verdict ('T4 passes, no modification needed') in the acceptance evidence — no code change. If the test DOES assert listening==true (contradicting research), relax that single assertion and note it explicitly (this is the ONLY permitted non-comment edit per delta PRD §6.3).
4. OUTPUT: A documented verdict (text/passed check) confirming T4's interaction with auto-stop is sound. If an assertion relaxation was needed, the modified test_idle_and_gpu.sh. Otherwise no file changes.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. If an assertion was relaxed, add a comment on the modified line explaining the auto-stop interaction.

subtasks.title: Run full pytest fast suite regression (confirm 197 tests still green)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified during research phase — the fast pytest suite currently has 197 tests across test_config.py (config defaults + TOML loading), test_config_repo_default.py (config.toml exposes all keys), test_feedback.py (record_final, notify_on_final, partial sync, throttle, atomic write), test_textproc.py (blocklist, whitespace, min-length), test_daemon.py (idle watchdog, toggle gate, CPU fallback, mic probe, logging), test_typing_backends.py, test_voicectl.py, test_control_socket.py. All 197 passed in 1.59s total. Run command: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short` (NOT the shell scripts — those are heavy GPU tests run separately).
2. INPUT: The complete tests/ directory after the P1.M1.T1.S1 comment fix.
3. LOGIC: Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/test_feed_audio.py --ignore=tests/e2e_virtual_mic.sh --ignore=tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh -v --tb=short` (the fast suite; the heavy shell-based tests require GPU cold init and are run explicitly). Confirm ALL tests pass. If any fail (which should not happen since the only change was a comment), investigate and report — a comment-only change cannot break a test unless the test parses config.toml comments (test_config_repo_default.py checks KEY VALUES, not comments, so it is safe).
4. OUTPUT: The pytest summary line (e.g., '197 passed in 1.59s') as regression evidence. No code changes expected.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B final sweep)
tasks.description: Per SOW §5, a final documentation sweep task verifies that cross-cutting/overview docs (README.md, top-level capability lists) are consistent with the changeset. The delta PRD §5 states Mode B 'does not apply' because README.md already documents all delta knobs (asr.auto_stop_idle_seconds, feedback.notify_on_final, the notification discipline). This task exists to let the implementing agent confirm that verdict rather than assume it, and to catch any doc drift introduced by the config.toml comment fix.

subtasks.title: Verify README.md documents all delta knobs and notification discipline correctly
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified during research phase — README.md (12,969 bytes) already documents: (a) asr.auto_stop_idle_seconds in its config tuning table with the 'forgotten hot-mic' framing, (b) feedback.notify_on_final in its config table, (c) the notification discipline (hypr_notify as master switch, ● listening / ✔ <text> / ■ stopped, partials go to state file only). The delta PRD §5 confirms: 'README.md already documents both new knobs... No cross-cutting README/overview sweep is warranted.' This subtask is a CONFIRMATION pass, not a rewrite.
2. INPUT: README.md at /home/dustin/projects/voice-typing/README.md, plus the completed P1.M1.T1.S1 (config.toml comment fix) and P1.M1.T2 (regression green).
3. LOGIC: Read README.md and grep for: 'auto_stop_idle_seconds', 'notify_on_final', 'hypr_notify', 'partial' (in the context of notification discipline). Confirm each delta knob is documented and the notification discipline matches the corrected config.toml comment (start/final/stop — NOT start/partial/final/stop). If README has any stale wording that contradicts the corrected config.toml, fix it (this is the catch-all). If README is already correct (expected per delta PRD §5), document that verdict — no edit needed.
4. OUTPUT: A documented verdict ('README.md verified correct, no edits needed' OR the specific edit made). If no edit, no file change.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level docs sweep. If README needed an edit, it rides with this subtask.
